name: a-short-history-of-nearly-everything
description: Bill Bryson's A Short History of Nearly Everything — popular science toolkit making the biggest questions about the universe, Earth, life, and science accessible, entertaining, and memorable
useCase: Understand the Big Bang, how Earth formed, how life evolved, how science works, and the building blocks of matter
knownRisks: Bryson is a journalist, not a scientist — some scientific explanations are simplified
The book was published in 2003 — some facts may be dated by newer discoveries
Bryson's humorous style may not appeal to readers who prefer more serious science writing
